- Standard markdown spec compliance

Establish UI/UX responsive design instructions & framework (Issue #195) (#198)

* docs: add comprehensive copilot instructions for UI/UX and API guidelines

- Design constants: breakpoints, test viewports, CSS variable names
- Responsive UI rules: mandatory CSS block inheritance, mobile-card tables, action buttons
- Theme support: light/dark mode requirements with no hardcoded colors
- Code reuse: macros/includes, shared JS/CSS, table row rendering
- API/backend: code reuse, security, limits/bounds, streaming, stable contracts
- Timezone support: UTC-at-rest requirement + client-side display conversion
- Python quality gate: auto-discovery linting + formatting command

Resolves #195

commit.message:
fix(ui+exec): spinner 1s, stealth URL cap, remove startup banner

core/scanner.py — 3 fixes:

1. Spinner interval 0.2s → 1.0s
   _run_with_progress spinner thread: time.sleep(0.2) → time.sleep(1.0)
   Em single mode, ui_update_status usa \r para sobrescrever a linha.
   0.2s = 5 updates/sec criava 5 linhas/seg quando copiado do terminal.
   1.0s = leitura humana confortável, terminal mais limpo.

2. Nuclei stealth URL cap (_NUCLEI_STEALTH_URL_THRESHOLD = 100)
   Quando valid_tdirs != [] E URL count > 100: nuclei_input_effective = recon_input
   (HTTPX live hosts, ex: 3 endpoints) em vez de nuclei_input (1336 crawled URLs)

   Motivo: http/misconfiguration, http/exposures, http/takeovers, http/default-logins
   sao verificacoes HOST-level (testam /admin, /backup, headers, etc.).
   Rodar contra 1336 URLs: mesmos checks × 1336 = duplicatas + explosao de requests.
   Rodar contra 3 hosts: mesmos findings, 99.8% menos requests.

   Antes:  1400 templates × 1336 URLs × 2.9 req/tmpl = 5.4M requests
   Depois: 1400 templates × 3 hosts  × 2.9 req/tmpl = ~12K requests

   Env var NUCLEI_STEALTH_URL_THRESHOLD (default 100) permite tunar o cap.
   Log: 'Stealth dir mode: 3 live hosts (skipping 1336 crawled URLs)'

3. _NUCLEI_STEALTH_URL_THRESHOLD constant adicionada ao bloco de constantes globais

main.py:
   Removido ui_clear() + ui_banner() redundante antes de init_seq() no main().
   O while-loop faz ui_clear() + ui_banner() na primeira iteracao de qualquer forma.
   Resultado: banner aparece 1x (com menu) em vez de 2x (startup + menu).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: UX fixes batch — memory editing, model settings precision, loading screen (#183, #184) (#186)

* fix(#183): use hardware tier for default context window (not raw 12GB threshold)

Replace the duplicated 12 GB totalRam check with hardwareProfileDetector.profile.recommendedMaxTokens.
ActivityManager.MemoryInfo.totalMem reports just under 12 GiB on physical devices
(kernel-reserved RAM excluded), so the old threshold silently fell through to 4096 on
12 GB devices like the S23 Ultra. The HardwareProfileDetector already applies a 10 GB
FLAGSHIP threshold and maps FLAGSHIP→8192, MID_RANGE→2048, LOW_POWER→1024.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#183): loading screen text colour on dark background

LoadingContent's Box uses a background modifier which does not update
LocalContentColor. The animated step Text and CircularProgressIndicator
inherited the parent content colour (black) and were invisible on the
dark background.

- Wrap Column in CompositionLocalProvider(LocalContentColor provides
  MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onBackground) so all child composables that
  inherit content colour render correctly.
- Add explicit color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onBackground to the
  animated step Text as belt-and-suspenders.
- Add explicit color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary to
  CircularProgressIndicator.
- Step counter Text already had onSurfaceVariant — left untouched.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#183): model settings sliders with editable number fields

Each SliderRow now renders a compact OutlinedTextField (76 dp) to the
right of the slider so users can type a precise value instead of
dragging.

- isInteger flag controls formatting (no decimals for context window;
  2 dp for temperature / top-p) and keyboard type (Number vs Decimal).
- Dragging the slider updates the text field in real time.
- Committing (keyboard Done) parses the text, clamps to the valid range,
  updates the slider, and calls onValueChangeFinished — same save path
  as the existing slider onValueChangeFinished.
- Invalid / empty text reverts to the current slider value on Done.
- focusManager.clearFocus() on Done dismisses the soft keyboard cleanly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#184): delete button in core memory edit sheet

The CoreMemoryDetailSheet only showed Cancel + Save. A Delete button
with destructive (error) colouring has been added on the left side of
the button row so the layout reads:

  [Delete]  ·  spacer  ·  [Cancel]  [Save]

Clicking Delete first shows an AlertDialog to confirm, then calls
viewModel.requestDeleteCoreMemory(memory.id) followed by
viewModel.closeCoreMemoryDetail() so the sheet dismisses immediately
while Room processes the deletion asynchronously.

Previously the episodic detail sheet was read-only with a single Close
button. It now matches the core memory sheet in all respects.

commit.message:
fix(nuclei): remove http/cves from core_dirs + drop -tags when dirs active

Problema: 13.8M requisicoes nuclei contra alvo DVWA/JuiceShop.

Causa raiz 1 — http/cves em core_dirs:
  get_nuclei_template_dirs() incluia 'http/cves' SEMPRE como core dir.
  http/cves contem 6000+ templates de CVE. Com 1336 endpoints:
    1336 URLs × 6000 templates × ~1.7 req/template = 13.6M requests
  O 'stealth mode' existe para RESTRINGIR o escopo, mas http/cves sozinho
  anulava todo o beneficio.

Fix: removido http/cves dos core_dirs. CVEs agora chegam apenas via
TECH_DIR_MAP (subdirs especificos: http/cves/php, http/cves/django, etc.)
que tem 10-50 templates cada — nao 6000.

Causa raiz 2 — -tags passado junto com -t dirs:
  run_nuclei passava -tags mesmo quando template_dirs estava ativo.
  Tags amplas como 'cve,xss,sqli' sao redundantes quando dirs ja definem
  o escopo e em algumas versoes do nuclei podem expandir alem dos dirs.

Fix: quando valid_tdirs for nao-vazio, nao passa -tags. Dirs sao o scope.
  -tags continua sendo passado no path de fallback (sem dirs detectados).

Estimativa de requests apos fix:
  http/misconfiguration (~200) + http/exposures (~150) + http/takeovers (~50)
  + http/default-logins (~100) + http/cves/php (~30) + http/cves/django (~5)
  = ~535 templates × 1336 URLs × ~1.5 req/template ≈ 1.07M requests (-92%)

commit.message:
fix: boolean false overrides dropped in multi-document helmfiles (#2527) (#2532)

* fix: environment values pollution causing boolean false overrides to be dropped (#2527)

PR #2367 introduced envCopy.Values = values in NewEnvironmentTemplateData,
where values = GetMergedValues() = Defaults + Values + CLIOverrides. This
caused .Environment.Values to include Defaults, so when multi-part helmfiles
re-assigned environment values via {{ toYaml .Environment.Values }}, Defaults
values (e.g. helmDefaults.atomic: true) were written into the environment
Values field. Later, GetMergedValues() applied Values over Defaults, causing
the stale atomic: true to win over the correct atomic: false override.

Fix: set .Environment.Values to Values + CLIOverrides only (excluding Defaults),
so re-assignment patterns don't pollute the Values layer with Defaults.
